compiled_state.title: Apply codebase exploration reliability techniques to reduce agent hallucinations across DSO workflows
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Context

Exploration agents across DSO workflows (debugging, brainstorming, onboarding) have shown hallucination patterns — reporting inferred findings as confirmed facts. Research identified actionable techniques: Chain-of-Verification (CoVe), confidence-informed self-consistency, static analysis integration, verification agent layers, and context budgeting. The /dso:onboarding epic (8fdd-a993) addresses this for onboarding specifically; this epic applies the same reliability techniques to other DSO workflows.

## Success Criteria

Placeholder — to be defined in a future /dso:brainstorm session.

## Research Findings (from 8fdd-a993 brainstorm)

Key techniques by effort level:
- Easy: filesystem grounding (verify before reporting), CoVe (generate+verify independently), confidence tagging
- Moderate: static analysis integration (AST parsers + LLM), repo map pattern (dependency-graph-ranked context), verification agent layer
- Architectural: multi-agent cross-validation, knowledge graph + agentic RAG, formal verification

Sources: arxiv 2509.18970 (agent hallucination taxonomy), ACL 2025 (CISC), Meta AI (CoVe), Aider repo map docs, CodeRabbit agentic validation, arxiv 2602.11988 (AGENTS.md evaluation)

## Dependencies

None
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Context
AI agents exploring the DSO codebase (during planning, investigation, and implementation) frequently miss cross-file dependencies because they rely on text-based grep, which cannot distinguish structural code patterns from comments or string literals. This leads to incomplete impact analysis during planning — the dominant contributor to cross-cutting interaction bugs (17 fix commits in the last month). ast-grep provides structural code search across 170+ languages including bash, enabling agents to find function definitions, callers, source-chain dependencies, and pattern usage with syntax awareness. ast-grep complements MCP-based semantic search (dso-soyt): ast-grep is deterministic structural pattern matching; semantic search is conceptual similarity retrieval.

## Success Criteria
- ast-grep is documented as a recommended dependency: installation instructions (brew install ast-grep on macOS, cargo install ast-grep --locked on Linux) in README and /dso:project-setup
- CLAUDE.md references sg as the preferred tool for structural code search when exploring cross-file dependencies, with specific example patterns
- Skills reference ast-grep in specific dependency discovery steps: implementation-plan Step 3 (file impact table), fix-bug Step 2 (investigation pre-loading), sprint Phase 3 (batch overlap context), brainstorm Phase 2 Step 2.5 (technical self-review). Each includes a command -v sg guard with Grep fallback.
- Graceful degradation: skills check command -v sg before use and fall back to Grep tool when ast-grep is not installed
- Agents demonstrably use sg commands during exploration — verified by reviewing session transcripts after rollout

## Dependencies
None. Complements dso-soyt (MCP semantic search).

## Approach
Tool-only integration: install ast-grep as a documented dependency, add CLAUDE.md references and skill guidance for agents to use sg commands during exploration. No wrapper scripts or deep integration into gates — purely agent-facing guidance with graceful fallback.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Done Definitions (lightweight preplanning)

- When this epic is complete, ast-grep installation instructions appear in README.md and /dso:project-setup skill, with platform-specific commands for macOS (brew) and Linux (cargo)
  ← Satisfies: "ast-grep is documented as a recommended dependency"
- When this epic is complete, CLAUDE.md contains sg as the preferred structural code search tool with at least 2 concrete example patterns (bash source chain, cross-file pattern match)
  ← Satisfies: "CLAUDE.md references sg as the preferred tool"
- When this epic is complete, implementation-plan Step 3, fix-bug Step 2, sprint Phase 3, and brainstorm Phase 2 Step 2.5 each contain ast-grep guidance with a command -v sg guard and Grep fallback
  ← Satisfies: "Skills reference ast-grep in specific dependency discovery steps"
- When this epic is complete, running any of the 4 updated skills on a system without ast-grep installed produces no errors and falls back to Grep-based search
  ← Satisfies: "Graceful degradation"
- When this epic is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Scope
- IN: README, CLAUDE.md, project-setup skill, 4 exploration-heavy skills (implementation-plan, fix-bug, sprint, brainstorm), graceful degradation
- OUT: Wrapper scripts, deep integration into gates/hooks, ast-grep pattern config files (those belong in 1a5b-35f9 centrality epic)

## Considerations
- [Reliability] ast-grep is an external binary — graceful degradation must be tested on systems without it
- [Maintainability] 7 files reference ast-grep; changes to sg CLI syntax require coordinated updates
- [Testing] Agent adoption validated by transcript review, not automated test
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
